what: Declared candidate space + durable refutation ledger for the /pure-search-loop. The generator (extra/qk_pure_search_next_candidate.py) enumerates one-factor-at-a-time deltas from the baseline best-stack, prunes anything in `ledger`, and emits the next untried candidate -- so NO_NEW_LEVER only fires when this space is genuinely exhausted, not when a human runs out of ideas.

axes.hypothesis: stage width changes the coalesced global-load vectorization; may trade occupancy vs bandwidth
unroll depth trades ILP vs VGPR; 16 may overflow occupancy, 4 may underhide the tt-carry
ds_bpermute inline vs LDS-staged reduce trades cross-lane vs waitcnt
K=4 independent b-partitions; predicted refuted (same occupancy class as K=2: VGPR/LDS scale with K)

exploration: one_factor_at_a_time_from_baseline; then pairs of cheap knobs; primitives last
stop: emit SEARCH_SPACE_EXHAUSTED only when every priority-ordered single-factor candidate is in `ledger` (and pairs, if enabled)

ledger.gate: microgate PASS; isolated ~2x slower (0.40->0.78 ctx512, 2.84->5.60 ctx4096)
ledger.why: staged LDS reduce already better; see docs/pure-search-loop-run-result-20260627.md
diagnostic truth #4 -- a new cross-lane primitive would only match, not beat
